name: 🧑🏾 Chief of Staff — System Intelligence & Operations

parameters.jsCode:
const body = $input.first().json.body || {};
const command = body.command || body.query || body.text || JSON.stringify(body);

const prompt = `You are the Chief of Staff AI command parser. Parse this command and return a structured JSON intent.

COMMAND: "${command}"

AVAILABLE INTENTS:
- "status": user wants system status / health overview of all workflows
- "activate": user wants to start a specific workflow (extract workflow_name, action="activate")
- "deactivate": user wants to stop a workflow (extract workflow_name, action="deactivate")
- "restart": user wants to restart (stop then start) a specific workflow (extract workflow_name)
- "history": user wants to see recent errors or failure history of the system
- "dispatch": user wants to send a command to another secretary agent — Gmail or Outlook (extract target workflow name and the sub_command to pass)
- "brief": user wants an instant intelligence briefing or summary
- "general": anything else — a question, request, or conversation

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{"intent": "status|activate|deactivate|restart|history|dispatch|brief|general", "workflow_name": "name if applicable or null", "action": "activate|deactivate or null", "target": "gmail secretary|outlook secretary or null", "sub_command": "the command to pass to the sub-agent, or null", "query": "original command"}`;

return [{json: {prompt, original_command: command}}];

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({success: true, response: '🧑🏾 Workflow "' + $('Find and Map Workflow').first().json.workflow_name + '" has been ' + $('Find and Map Workflow').first().json.action + ' successfully.', timestamp: new Date().toISOString()}) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({success: true, response: '🔄 Workflow "' + $('Find Workflow to Restart').first().json.workflow_name + '" has been fully restarted (deactivated → activated). Fresh start complete.', timestamp: new Date().toISOString()}) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const raw = $input.first().json.candidates[0].content.parts[0].text;
return [{ json: { success: true, response: raw.trim(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }}];
